description_long: This object is a stylized helicopter with a long, slender fuselage and a prominent main rotor assembly at the top. The body is primarily blue with gold and gray accents, and the main rotor hub is circular with four angular extensions resembling blades. The tail is extremely elongated and thin, ending in a small circular detail, and the helicopter lacks a visible tail rotor. The landing gear is not traditional skids but rather a flat, cross-shaped base integrated into the lower fuselage. No cockpit windows or doors are visible, giving the object a highly abstract and ornamental appearance. The overall design is more symbolic than functional, with exaggerated proportions and minimal surface detail.
description: A stylized blue and gold helicopter with a long, thin tail and a circular main rotor hub, lacking visible cockpit windows or traditional landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view shows the circular main rotor hub and cross-shaped base, with the long tail extending backward.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated, thin tail and the flat, angular main body with gold and blue coloring.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the end of the tail and the symmetrical cross-shaped base, with minimal detail on the tail tip.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the long tail and the angular, stylized main body.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four angular, short blades extending from a circular blue hub, appearing metallic with gold tips; blades are stylized and not functional.
attribute_tail_rotor: Not visible; the tail is a long, thin rod with no discernible tail rotor or blades.
attribute_landing_skids: No traditional landing skids; instead, a flat, cross-shaped base is present, colored blue and gold, appearing metallic.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Not visible; no cockpit windows or transparent sections are present on the body.
